key_themes: Asset fire sale following cash burn and clinical setbacks
Failure to commercialize Cell Squeeze cell therapy platform
Massive layoffs, leadership changes, and ultimate shutdown
Negligible ongoing investor interest in residual OTC ticker
summary: SQZ Biotechnologies (SQZB) effectively ceased operations after shareholders approved a plan of liquidation and asset sale in early 2024, with the company filing for dissolution shortly thereafter. Once a high-profile MIT spinout with innovative cell therapy technology, major Roche partnerships, and significant VC backing, SQZ burned through cash without achieving commercialization, leading to an 80-person layoff and wind-down. In 2026 the residual OTC ticker trades at ~$0.02–0.024 with tiny volume and almost no meaningful investor discussion, representing a total loss for late-stage shareholders and a classic biotech cautionary tale.[[1]](https://www.fiercebiotech.com/biotech/sqz-months-are-laying-80-staff-opts-liquidation-and-118m-asset-fire-sale-stemcell)[[2]](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20240229047788/en/Asset-Sale-and-Plan-of-Liquidation-and-Dissolution-Approved-by-SQZB-Shareholders)[[3]](https://robinhood.com/us/en/stocks/SQZB/)
sources: 10+ web/news results (primarily 2024 liquidation announcements and historical coverage), Limited X/social activity (fewer than 5 relevant recent posts; mostly one podcast mention amid irrelevant spam)
